content.full_text:
September 22, 1977

Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.
Dobbs, Doyle and Nielsen
One Maritime Plaza, Suite 2500
San Francisco, California 94111

Dear Chip:

Thank you for your letter of June 20, 1977 regarding an apparent
conflict in advice issued with respect to how a lobbyist employer
should report expenses incurred by a lobbyist and paid for by the
employer.

I advised Mr. Joe A. Gonsalves on May 19, 1977, that a lobbyist
employer should report in a lump sum, as payments in support or
assistance of a lobbyist, expenses paid by the employer that were
incurred by the lobbyist. This advice was based on Section 86109(c)
of the Political Reform Act which states that the periodic reports
of lobbyist employers shall contain "the total amount of payments
to influence legislative and administrative action this period"
and opinion 75-067 (1IFPPC OPS 145) which states:

"The employer of a lobbyist must report the amount of
payments to influence legislative or administrative
action... However, the itemization required in the
lobbyist's report need not be duplicated in the
employer's report."

As you pointed out in your letter, our Lobbyist Bulletin published
on July 19, 1976 contained the following advice: "Once an expense
has been disclosed on the lobbyist's report it need not be disclosed
again on the employer's report." Although this language was not
as explicit as it might have been, it was intended to suggest that
expenses incurred by a lobbyist need not be itemized on the lobbyist
employer's report, but that the payment of the expenses must still
be disclosed by the employer.

go Nielsen
age 2
pt. 22, 1977

A clarification of this advice is planned for the next Lobbyist
Bulletin which is scheduled to be published soon. Because of
the apparent confusion of lobbyist employers regarding this matter,
reporting by employers in the manner I have described will be re-
quired prospectively only from the time the clarification appears
in the Lobbyist Bulletin.

If you would like to discuss the matter further, or wish clarifi-
cation, do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,


Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative

AH:bw

DOBBS, DOYLE & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111

TELEPHONE (415) 362-1940

June 20, 1977


Mr. Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Alan:

     In reading the advice you issued in your letter of May 19, 1977 to Mr. Joe Gonsalves, I noticed that it differed from the information published in your FPPC Lobbyist Bulletin (Vol. 1, No. 1) of July 19, 1976.

     You advised Mr. Gonsalves that when a lobbyist incurs expenses charged on the employer's credit card and reports those expenses on his periodic lobbyist report, the employer need not duplicate the itemization required in the lobbyist report but must, however, "report the payments to the credit card company in lump sum as payments in support or assistance of a lobbyist on Page 2 of the Form 650." In its discussion of reporting requirements for expenses incurred by the lobbyist but paid by the employer, the aforementioned Lobbyist Bulletin (Page 2, 2c) states, however, that "once an expense has been disclosed on the lobbyist's report it need not be disclosed again on the employer's report."

     We bring this to your attention since we have been counseling clients with the advice contained in your Lobbyist Bulletin.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Vido G. Nielsen, Jr.

VGN:aeo

SACRAMENTO OFFICE · SUITE 800, 1006 FOURTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA. 95814 · (916) 443-2051

sections.conclusion: The employer of a lobbyist must report the amount of payments to influence legislative or administrative action in lump sum as payments in support or assistance of a lobbyist, but need not duplicate the itemization required in the lobbyist's report. Expenses incurred by a lobbyist need not be itemized on the lobbyist employer's report, but the payment of the expenses must still be disclosed by the employer. This reporting requirement will be enforced prospectively only from the time clarification appears in the next Lobbyist Bulletin.
sections.question_synthetic: How should lobbyist employers report expenses incurred by lobbyists and paid for by the employer—must they itemize these expenses or can they report them in lump sum, and does prior guidance in the Lobbyist Bulletin conflict with newer advice?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC clarifies that lobbyist employers must report payments for lobbyist expenses in lump sum without duplicating the itemization in the lobbyist's report, citing Section 86109(c) and Opinion 75-057. The apparently conflicting language in the July 1976 Lobbyist Bulletin will be clarified in an upcoming bulletin, and the corrected reporting requirement will apply prospectively only.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is response letter dated September 22, 1977 from FPPC Compliance Representative Alan Herndon to Virgo Nielsen. Includes incoming request letter dated June 20, 1977 as context. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present; question and conclusion synthesized from body text. Extraction confidence reduced due to OCR quality issues and somewhat informal letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should lobbyist employers report expenses incurred by lobbyists and paid for by the employer—must they itemize these expenses or can they report them in lump sum, and does prior guidance in the Lobbyist Bulletin conflict with newer advice?

CONCLUSION: The employer of a lobbyist must report the amount of payments to influence legislative or administrative action in lump sum as payments in support or assistance of a lobbyist, but need not duplicate the itemization required in the lobbyist's report. Expenses incurred by a lobbyist need not be itemized on the lobbyist employer's report, but the payment of the expenses must still be disclosed by the employer. This reporting requirement will be enforced prospectively only from the time clarification appears in the next Lobbyist Bulletin.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to apparent conflict between advice issued to Joe Consalves on May 19, 1977 regarding lobbyist employer expense reporting and guidance published in the July 1976 Lobbyist Bulletin. The FPPC clarifies that employers must report payments for lobbyist-incurred expenses in lump sum without itemization, though the payment itself must be disclosed, and announces prospective enforcement of this requirement.
